title: grap-target
desc: How to construct a verified, bash-safe grap target string using a11y find. Output must include # FOCUS for the current focused leaf path, and # TARGET as the primary paste-ready hwnd:0x... token plus JSON5 context. Grap output must be shell-friendly, and copy-paste verification is part of the default contract. Covers why {a,b} brace expansion breaks in bash and how hwnd-first format avoids it.
steps: Run a11y find <pattern> and treat all fields title/proc/domain/cmd/url as matchable
Always print # FOCUS when the focused leaf can affect interaction, even if unrelated to the target (for example modal/error popups)

Print ## TARGET section (stdout, bold cyan). Format (v5.13+):
## TARGET
"{hwnd:0x...,pid:...,proc:'chrome',domain:'chatgpt.com'}#absTagPath"
wkappbot a11y find "..."
[OK] Nms
WindowTitle → stderr (not stdout — prevents noise from unrelated tab names)

Mandatory field order in grap JSON5: hwnd first, pid second (collision safety), proc, then domain/file/cls/matchedField.
matchedField auto-injected: if search matched via cmd/title/uia, that field+snippet appended (e.g. cmd:'...' when chatgpt.com search matched VS Code via cmdline).
Grap output must be shell-friendly: a pasted target string should work without manual editing, quoting, or brace escaping
Always verify pasted grap targets by re-running a11y find on the emitted hwnd:0x... form; [OK] means the pasted token resolves back to the same hwnd

Multiple windows match → ## TARGETS N matches header (outer loop in A11yCommand), then one ## TARGET block per window — each with its own paste grap + command line + [OK] Nms.
Do NOT use --all or --nth to force: show all blocks, let user pick specific grap.
Each block's grap has matchedField injected so the reason for match is explicit.

OUTPUT FORMAT (v5.13+): Each section wrapped in code fence for markdown compatibility. FOCUS section:
## FOCUS
```
"{hwnd:...}#absTagPath"
[OK] Nms
```
TARGET section:
## WindowTitle
```
"{hwnd:...,proc:...,cmd:'chatgpt.com'}#absTagPath"
wkappbot a11y find "..."
[OK] Nms  ← cmd: chatgpt.com
```
Multiple matches: ### TARGETS N matches header before first block. Blank line between blocks. Window title in ## heading (not in grap). matchedField annotation on [OK] line (e.g. ← cmd: chatgpt.com).

OUTPUT FORMAT (v5.13+): Each section wrapped in code fence for markdown compatibility. FOCUS section:
## FOCUS
```
"{hwnd:...}#absTagPath"
[OK] Nms
```
TARGET section:
## WindowTitle
```
"{hwnd:...,proc:...,cmd:'chatgpt.com'}#absTagPath"
wkappbot a11y find "..."
[OK] Nms  ← cmd: chatgpt.com
```
Multiple matches: ### TARGETS N matches header before first block. Blank line between blocks. Window title in ## heading (not in grap). matchedField annotation on [OK] line (e.g. ← cmd: chatgpt.com).

WINDOWLESS PROCESS SCAN: After EnumWindows, scan all processes for cmdLine matches. Walk PPID chain (NativeMethods.GetParentProcessId via NtQueryInformationProcess.Reserved3) to find host window (e.g. VS Code terminal running chatgpt.com session). MatchedVia='cmd', MatchedSnippet=matching token only (not full cmdline). context matches (foreground heuristic) filtered out of find results.
